condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I began by fetching the Metaculus question details (post_id 43742) to understand the exact resolution criteria: YES if "Google" or "Gemini" (case-insensitive) is spoken aloud or appears as visible on-screen text during the official WWDC 2026 opening keynote on June 8, 2026. Incidental appearances in URLs or generic home-screen mockups don't count, but clear references to Google the company, a Google product, or "Gemini" the AI model do count.

I then ran three parallel research queries covering: (1) what to expect at WWDC 2026 regarding the Google/Gemini partnership, (2) Apple's reluctance to mention Google branding, and (3) historical WWDC keynote mentions of ChatGPT/OpenAI/Google. This returned comprehensive cached research from multiple sources (MacRumors, TechCrunch, AppleInsider, 9to5Mac, Bloomberg, Wikipedia).

I followed up with multiple web searches and URL fetches to get the most recent May–June 2026 reporting, including:
- **MacRumors (May 28, 2026)**: Apple plans to emphasize on-device AI as a key WWDC focus
- **MacRumors (May 5, 2026)**: iOS 27 will let users choose between ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini via an "Extensions" system
- **Newsweek**: WWDC 2026 comprehensive preview confirming Extensions feature
- **felloai.com**: WWDC 2026 preview noting Apple's genai.apple.com subdomain registration
- **implicator.ai**: Analysis of Gemini distillation shaping Apple's WWDC AI push
- **Engadget**: WWDC 2026 expectations

I also searched for the WWDC 2025 keynote transcript to verify whether "Google" was mentioned, and searched for WWDC 2024 ChatGPT mention counts.

## Key Evidence

**FOR YES:**
- **100% base rate**: Apple mentioned "ChatGPT" and "OpenAI" on stage at both WWDC 2024 and WWDC 2025 when those were AI partners
- **iOS 27 Extensions system**: Bloomberg and MacRumors confirm users will choose between ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini — demoing this would show brand names on-screen
- **Low resolution bar**: Question requires only spoken OR on-screen text; on-screen text in UI mockups is very hard to avoid in a 2-hour keynote
- **$1B/year deal publicly announced**: Joint Apple-Google statement in January 2026; no secrecy around the partnership
- **"Gemini" vs "Google" distinction**: Apple may avoid "Google" (advertising association) but could show "Gemini" (model name, less baggage); either word resolves YES

**FOR NO:**
- **Apple's absolute control of pre-recorded keynotes**: Every frame scripted and edited
- **Reported reluctance to highlight Google** due to privacy branding concerns
- **On-device AI emphasis**: Apple positioning local processing as competitive advantage, potentially undermining cloud partnership narrative
- **"Apple Foundation Models" branding** exists precisely to avoid crediting Google
- **WWDC 2025 precedent**: Apple did NOT mention "Google" in spoken keynote narration (though Google wasn't an AI partner then)
- **ChatGPT precedent may be weaker than it appears**: ChatGPT was the *only* third-party option; with multiple providers in Extensions, Apple could demo generically

**Factor correlation issue**: I recognized that the YES factors (precedent, Extensions, low bar, Extensions being flagship) are correlated — if Apple deliberately avoids naming providers, all fail simultaneously. This justified discounting from ~95% factor-implied probability to **80%**.

## Conclusion

I concluded at **80% probability YES**. The strongest mechanism is the iOS 27 Extensions feature that lets users choose between named AI providers including Gemini — demonstrating this on stage would almost certainly show "Gemini" on screen. Combined with the 100% base rate of Apple naming AI partners at recent WWDCs and the low resolution bar (spoken OR on-screen), the evidence strongly favors YES. The 20% NO probability accounts for Apple's tight control over keynote content, their demonstrated reluctance to highlight Google, and the possibility they demo Extensions generically or save it for non-keynote sessions.

capability_gaps: No access to actual WWDC 2025 keynote transcript to verify whether 'Google' appeared on screen (only claims about spoken narration available)
No prediction market data checked (Polymarket, Kalshi, Manifold) — could have provided useful calibration signal
subtle_bugs: The research tool returned cached data from April 2026, missing May-June developments. The agent compensated with web_search but should note the staleness window.

The WWDC 2025 claim that Apple 'did NOT mention Google in spoken keynote narration' appears to come from the question background itself, not independently verified — potential circular reasoning risk.

classification.approach: Base rate from WWDC 2024/2025 AI partner mention precedent, updated with iOS 27 Extensions reporting and Apple branding pattern analysis.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether Apple will deliberately choose to demo the iOS 27 Extensions feature with named providers visible on screen, or present it generically to avoid crediting Google.

risk_flags.evidence: Agent did not check any prediction markets (Polymarket, Kalshi, Manifold) for this question despite them potentially having active markets on WWDC content. No community prediction was available on Metaculus either (num_forecasters: 2, community_prediction: null).

Agent claims '100% base rate' of Apple mentioning AI partners at WWDC, based on only 2 data points (WWDC 2024 and 2025 with ChatGPT). A 2/2 sample is extremely small for establishing a base rate, and the reference class (ChatGPT as user-facing optional feature) differs materially from Gemini's role as a foundation model provider.

The agent's own research found that the Gemini integration is fundamentally different from ChatGPT — Gemini powers the foundation models while ChatGPT was a user-facing option. Agent noted this in the premortem counterargument but may have under-weighted it. Apple routinely does not credit infrastructure suppliers on stage (e.g., TSMC for chip manufacturing, Samsung for displays).

strengths: Thorough multi-source research: agent ran 3 parallel research queries plus 6 web searches and 8 URL fetches, covering historical WWDC precedent, current iOS 27 Extensions reporting, and Apple branding strategy

Explicitly identified and quantified factor correlation: recognized that YES factors (precedent, Extensions, low bar) fail simultaneously if Apple deliberately avoids naming, and correctly discounted from 95% factor-implied to 80%

Strong premortem with substantive counterargument: identified that ChatGPT precedent is weaker than it appears because ChatGPT was the only option vs Extensions having multiple providers, and that Apple Foundation Models branding exists to avoid crediting Google
Correctly parsed the low resolution bar (spoken OR on-screen text) as a key structural factor pushing probability higher
Good distinction between 'Google' (company with ad baggage) and 'Gemini' (model name, less baggage) — both resolve YES, making avoidance harder
Identified the Extensions UI as the strongest specific mechanism for YES resolution, grounded in Bloomberg/MacRumors reporting from May 2026
weaknesses: Did not check prediction markets (Polymarket, Kalshi, Manifold) which could have provided calibration signal from betting markets
Base rate of 2/2 WWDC mentions is too small a sample to treat as a strong 100% base rate; agent acknowledged the discount but the anchor framing as '100% base rate' may have anchored too high

Did not attempt to access or search the actual WWDC 2025 keynote transcript on Rev.com (found in search results) to verify the claim about Google not being mentioned — instead relied on the question's own background info, risking circular reasoning
The research tool's cached data was from April 2026, missing a full month of developments; while web_search compensated, this staleness wasn't explicitly flagged as a limitation
Did not explore the infrastructure supplier analogy (Apple doesn't credit TSMC, Samsung, etc. on stage) which could argue for a lower probability than 80%

issues: Bloomberg URL fetch returned HTTP 403 (paywall) — mitigated by secondary sources but represents a recurring data access gap
Research tool returned cached data from April 2026 (stale by ~2 months), requiring supplementary web searches to get current reporting

actionable_improvements: Always check prediction markets (Polymarket, Kalshi, Manifold) for questions with likely active markets, especially for high-profile technology events
When a base rate comes from only 2 data points, explicitly flag the sample size limitation and widen the confidence interval rather than framing it as '100% base rate'

When search results surface a full keynote transcript (e.g., Rev.com for WWDC 2025), fetch and search it to independently verify claims from the question background rather than relying on potentially circular information
Consider infrastructure supplier analogies for foundation model partnerships — Apple not crediting TSMC/Samsung on stage is a relevant reference class that was overlooked
Flag research tool cache staleness explicitly when the data is more than 2 weeks old relative to a time-sensitive question
